categories.name: DPA Parties And Processing Scope
categories.definition: Obligations to identify the parties to the data processing arrangement and describe the basic scope of the processing. This includes the controller and processor identities and contact details, the duration, nature and purpose of processing, and the categories and types of personal data and data subjects.

categories.name: Security Of Processing Measures
categories.definition: Obligations concerning technical and organisational safeguards for protecting personal data and assessing security risk. This includes implementing, maintaining, testing, and evaluating security measures, confidentiality controls, resilience, restoration, and risk-based security assessment.

categories.name: Breach Notification And Breach Content
categories.definition: Obligations to notify personal data breaches and to include specified information in breach notices or records. This covers notification to authorities, controllers, and data subjects, as well as the required content and documentation of breaches.

categories.name: Data Protection Impact Assessment
categories.definition: Obligations relating to conducting, consulting on, reviewing, or documenting a data protection impact assessment. This includes the DPIA’s required contents, necessity and proportionality analysis, risk assessment, mitigation measures, and related consultation or review duties.

categories.name: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
categories.definition: Obligations governing the appointment, change, control, and contractual flow-down of sub-processors. This includes prior authorization, notice of changes, objection rights, and ensuring sub-processors are bound by equivalent obligations.

categories.name: Processing Instructions And Lawful Direction
categories.definition: Obligations requiring the processor and its personnel to act only on the controller’s documented instructions, and to handle situations where law requires different processing. This includes informing the controller when instructions appear unlawful and restricting processing by authorized persons to controller instructions.

categories.name: Confidentiality Of Personnel
categories.definition: Obligations to ensure that persons authorized to process personal data are bound by confidentiality. This includes contractual commitments or statutory confidentiality duties for personnel with access to personal data.

categories.name: Assistance To Controller
categories.definition: Obligations requiring the processor to support the controller in meeting GDPR duties. This includes assistance with data subject rights, security, breach notification, DPIA compliance, and prior consultation with supervisory authorities.

categories.name: Data Return Deletion And End-Of-Service Handling
categories.definition: Obligations that apply when processing services end, including returning or deleting personal data. This category covers post-service handling of data after the provision of processing services concludes.

categories.name: Compliance Reporting And Audit Cooperation
categories.definition: Obligations to provide evidence of compliance and cooperate with oversight activities. This includes making information available to demonstrate compliance and allowing or contributing to audits and inspections.

categories.name: International Transfer Restrictions
categories.definition: Obligations restricting transfers of personal data to third countries or international organizations unless authorized by the controller.

categories.name: Codes Of Conduct And Certification
categories.definition: Obligations or permissions relating to demonstrating compliance through approved codes of conduct or certification mechanisms.

categories.name: Controller Rights Over Processing Arrangements
categories.definition: Rights reserved to the controller to control or stop processing arrangements. This includes rights to object to sub-processor changes, suspend processing, or terminate the data processing agreement in specified cases.

categories.name: Controller Breach Response Duties
categories.definition: Obligations imposed on the controller after becoming aware of a personal data breach. This includes notifying supervisory authorities, documenting breaches, and communicating breaches to data subjects where required.

categories.name: Controller DPIA And Consultation Duties
categories.definition: Obligations imposed on the controller in relation to DPIAs and related consultation. This includes carrying out the DPIA, seeking advice from the DPO, seeking views of data subjects, and reviewing processing against the DPIA when risks change.

categories.name: Liability For Processing Damage
categories.definition: Obligations or rules allocating liability for damage caused by processing. This includes liability standards for processors and controllers where GDPR obligations are breached or processing is unlawful.

R23: Data Return Deletion And End-Of-Service Handling
